experiments.reasoning: The passage describes a deliberate procedure involving the use of a sealed thermometer (thermoscope) to measure heat transfer from a luminous object (likely a candle or similar light source). The experiment involves applying the thermoscope to the shining parts of the object and observing whether the spirit of wine inside warms significantly.
experiments.description: The experiment involved taking a luminous part of a bone (possibly from a neck of veal) and using a sealed thermoscope with tinted spirit of wine to test whether the light emitted by the bone could transfer heat to the spirit. The experimenter marked the shining parts and applied the thermoscope to see if the liquid warmed.
experiments.quote: I applied a seal’d Weather glass, furnished with tincted spirit of wine, for a pretty while, and could not satisfy my self, that the shining parts did at all sensibly warm the liquor: But the Thermoscope, though good in its kind, being not fitted for such nice Experiments, I did not build much upon that tryal.
experiments.outcome: Null (the experimenter did not find significant warming of the spirit of wine).
